channel.title: Crime on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Crime on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Miss Violet Speaks — Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: Miss Violet is proud of the Prime Minister, concerned about the quarry, and has a few structured thoughts on the young man who went directly back to prison.

item.title: Bajan Bugle — Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: Mottley wants Barbados to host the global Borrowers' Platform secretariat. QEH confirms its financials are fine. A man went straight back to prison.

item.title: Trini Brief – Auntie Cheryl: Tuesday, April 14, 2026
item.description: Auntie Cheryl from Chaguanas is very proud of the PM, concerned about the murder rate, and has already bought tickets to Metamorphosis. Tuesday's dispatch.

item.title: Trini Brief – Trini Dispatch: Tuesday, April 14, 2026
item.description: Trinidad skipped the CARICOM emergency meeting it demanded, crime is 45% up, and the Prime Minister is still in a one-woman war with the entire Caribbean. Tuesday's dispatch.

item.title: Daily Brief — Friday, April 10, 2026
item.description: Oil revenues surge past $159B in Q1, Ali eyes Gulf storage deals, police graduate 101 new recruits, and the digital traffic registry is coming whether you want it or not.

item.title: Jamaica Brief — Cousin Leroy, April 10, 2026
item.description: Leroy in the Bronx has thoughts about Shanoya, the soldier situation, gas station robberies, and why Jamaica keep making him proud and worried at the same time.

item.title: Jamaica Brief — Yard Report, April 10, 2026
item.description: JDF soldier charged with girlfriend's murder, bartender shot in St. Elizabeth, Shanoya Douglas has world leads, and an MP has been summoned. Another Friday in Jamaica.

item.title: Trini Brief — Trini Dispatch, April 10, 2026
item.description: A seven-year-old is dead at Pigeon Point, the government is sending a delegation to Caracas, PM Persad-Bissessar distributed fire tenders in Penal, and the PNM Tobago council internal election has been rescheduled. Standard.

item.title: Guyana Daily Brief – Wednesday, April 8, 2026
item.description: Guyana pockets US$761M in oil revenue, denies secret payout to gas contractor, and a cop on video says 'I'll kill you.' Wednesday, April 8.

item.title: The Yard Report – April 8, 2026
item.description: Kingston's sardonic take on Jamaica's minus-one-percent growth, students exposed to sex in school shelters, and a JDF soldier charged with murder.

item.title: The Yard Report — Jamaica Brief, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: Jamaica's weekly news with the bark left on. JACDEN scandal, fuel prices up 20%, and Jamaica still cleaning up after Melissa.

item.title: The Trini Dispatch — Trinidad & Tobago Brief, Monday April 6, 2026
item.description: T&T just elected Kamla. The dust is settling, the promises are flying, and a cyclist died at Easter Prix. The Trini Dispatch has the full picture.

item.title: Daily Brief — Tuesday, March 31, 2026
item.description: Oil hits 918K bpd, Georgetown floods, GUYOIL raises fuel prices, a teen is stabbed in Lacytown, and Uncle Sam wants his tax treaty. Your Tuesday briefing.

item.title: Guyana Daily Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
item.description: Government grabs 22 Georgetown streets, Exxon builds an 8th oil ship without approval, and Kristi Noem visits State House. Plus: a rubber duck, a stolen $7M, and Uncle Ramesh has thoughts.

item.title: ☕ Monday Brief: Budget Debate D-Day, Mohameds Ruling Today, and Georgetown Gets a Fancy New Hotel
item.description: Budget 2026 debate opens in the National Assembly, the Acting CJ rules on the Mohameds extradition challenge TODAY, Georgetown’s newest hotel opens, Mashramani gets $120M, and cattle rustlers allegedly killed a Rupununi conservationist. Your Monday morning news circus from all four papers.

item.title: ☕ Sunday Brief: Budget Debate Opens Monday, Cement Truck Kills on Demerara Bridge, and the Government Discovers 'Late Movers' Advantage'
item.description: Budget 2026 debate starts Monday while Finance Minister discovers Guyana has a ’late movers’ advantage’ in oil. Meanwhile, an unlicensed driver kills someone on the Demerara Bridge and the opposition says Tabatinga school is nowhere near ready. Your 5-minute Sunday news circus.

item.title: Wednesday Brief: Speaker Nadir Says Electing 'International Fugitive' Would Stain Parliament, 70kg Cocaine Bust, Belgium Wants to Build Our Ports
item.description: Speaker Nadir drops the ‘international fugitive’ bomb on WIN, 70kg of cocaine found in Parika, Belgium wants to help build our ports, IATA talks airport improvements, and AG Nandlall schools Charrandass on what ‘fugitive offender’ actually means.

item.title: ☕ Monday Brief: Former Finance Minister Admits Exxon Exploited Us, Venezuela Border Watch, and Pay-To-Party Culture
item.description: Winston Jordan finally admits what everyone knew, PM Phillips says troops are ready for anything Venezuelan, and birthday parties now cost admission.

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Monday Brief: Crime Stats, Search & Rescue Upgrade, and Traffic Chaos
item.description: Murders up 11% despite overall crime drop, new $123M search and rescue system launched, over 3,100 traffic offences in one week, and financial system reforms promised.

item.title: 😊 [PRO-GOV] Uncle Ramesh: When De Numbers Tell De REAL Story
item.description: Uncle Ramesh explains why 25% crime reduction is actually amazing, and why spending $123M on safety makes sense. Progress is progress!
